description: Greenfield Deno rebuild of Sapling — a branching chat app with mindmap visualization. Full feature parity with README.md. Targets Deno Deploy with Turso. Stack: Fresh, Hono, Drizzle, Vercel AI SDK, D3.js, TanStack, Ark UI, UnoCSS, jose, Zod.

userStories.title: Fresh project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Fresh project in versions/deno-vs/ so all subsequent stories have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: versions/deno-vs/ contains deno.json, fresh.config.ts, main.ts, dev.ts
Standard Fresh directory structure exists: routes/, islands/, components/, static/
deno task dev starts the dev server without errors
Default Fresh index route renders a page in the browser
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scaffolded using Fresh 2 (deno run -A jsr:@fresh/init). Fresh 2 uses vite.config.ts instead of fresh.config.ts, and does not use dev.ts — the dev server runs via `deno task dev` which calls vite directly.

userStories.title: Hono API handler wired under /api/*
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Hono mounted as the API handler for all /api/* routes in Fresh so backend routes can be added as Hono route handlers.
acceptanceCriteria: Hono is added as a dependency in deno.json imports
A Fresh catch-all route (routes/api/[...path].ts) delegates all /api/* requests to a Hono app instance
A health check route GET /api/health returns { ok: true } with status 200
Fetching /api/health in the browser returns { ok: true }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Hono app instance lives in api/app.ts and uses .basePath('/api'). The catch-all route routes/api/[...path].ts uses define.handlers() function form (RouteHandler union type supports HandlerFn). The old routes/api/[name].tsx scaffold was removed.

userStories.title: Drizzle ORM + Turso database connection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Drizzle ORM configured with a Turso (libSQL) connection so the app has a typed database client ready for schema definition and queries.
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle-orm and @libsql/client are added as dependencies in deno.json imports
db/client.ts exports a Drizzle client instance using TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN env vars
drizzle.config.ts exists at the project root with the correct dialect and schema path
TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN are documented in .env.example
Importing db/client.ts does not throw at startup (env vars may be empty strings in dev)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses @libsql/client/node (not the default /web) because Deno selects the web implementation which doesn't support file: URLs. The /node path uses native bindings and supports file:./sapling.db for local dev. drizzle-kit added for drizzle.config.ts type support. @libsql/client/node mapped as a separate import in deno.json. *.db added to .gitignore.

userStories.title: UnoCSS styling and Biome linting setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need UnoCSS for Tailwind-compatible utility classes and Biome for linting and formatting so the codebase stays consistent from the start.
acceptanceCriteria: UnoCSS is configured in fresh.config.ts as a Fresh plugin
Tailwind preset is enabled so standard tw utility classes work (e.g. flex, text-sm, bg-white)
biome.json exists at the project root with sensible defaults (format on save, no unused vars)
deno task lint runs Biome with zero errors on the initial scaffold
A UnoCSS utility class applied to the index route renders correctly in the browser
AUTH_JWT_SECRET and SAPLING_DEFAULT_MODEL are also documented in .env.example
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: UnoCSS configured in vite.config.ts (not fresh.config.ts — Fresh 2 uses Vite). Uses presetWind for Tailwind-compatible classes. virtual:uno.css imported in client.ts. Biome 1.9.4 added as npm dep; binary in node_modules/.bin/biome after `deno install --allow-scripts=npm:@biomejs/biome`. deno task lint runs ./node_modules/.bin/biome check. AUTH_JWT_SECRET and SAPLING_DEFAULT_MODEL added to .env.example.

userStories.title: Database schema and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full Drizzle schema for all tables so the app has a typed, migrated data layer before any feature work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: db/schema.ts defines tables: user (id, email, password_hash, created_at), user_api_key (user_id, provider, encrypted_key, created_at), chat (id, user_id, title, default_model, created_at), node (id, chat_id, parent_id, role, content, created_at), node_metadata (node_id, provider, model, temperature, tools_called, files, token_count)
role is defined as a Drizzle enum with values: user, assistant, system
node.parent_id is nullable (null = root node of the chat)
Indexes exist on node(chat_id), node(parent_id), node_metadata(provider), node_metadata(model)
All Drizzle inferred types (InferSelectModel, InferInsertModel) are exported from db/schema.ts
deno task db:migrate generates and applies migrations without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: db/schema.ts defines all 5 tables with Drizzle sqlite-core. Role enum via text+enum option. node.parent_id nullable. Indexes on node(chat_id), node(parent_id), node_metadata(provider), node_metadata(model). All InferSelectModel/InferInsertModel types exported. drizzle.config.ts updated to use process.env (runs in Node.js via drizzle-kit) and excluded from Deno typecheck via deno.json exclude array. db:generate task = node drizzle-kit generate; db:migrate task = deno run -A db/migrate.ts using drizzle-orm/libsql/migrator.

userStories.title: User registration endpoint
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to create an account with an email and password so I can access my chats.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/signup accepts { email: string, password: string }
Zod validates the body; returns 400 with { error: string } on invalid input
Password is hashed using Deno's built-in crypto (PBKDF2 or equivalent) — never stored plain
On success: user row is inserted, a signed JWT is returned in an httpOnly cookie, and { id, email } is returned in the body
Returns 409 with { error: 'Email already registered' } if email is taken
JWT is signed using AUTH_JWT_SECRET env var via the jose library
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: zod and jose added as npm deps. lib/hash.ts implements PBKDF2 via Web Crypto API (hashPassword/verifyPassword). api/routes/auth.ts implements POST /api/auth/signup with Zod validation, duplicate email check, password hashing, user insert, JWT signing (HS256, 7d expiry), and httpOnly cookie. api/app.ts mounts authRouter at /auth.

userStories.title: Sign-in, sign-out, and auth middleware
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want to sign in and sign out securely. As a developer, I need auth middleware that protects all routes requiring a logged-in user.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/signin accepts { email, password }; returns { id, email } and sets httpOnly JWT cookie on success
POST /api/auth/signin returns 401 with { error: 'Invalid credentials' } on wrong password
POST /api/auth/signout clears the auth cookie and returns 200
GET /api/auth/me returns { id, email } for authenticated requests, or 401 for unauthenticated
A requireAuth Hono middleware exists in lib/auth.ts that reads the JWT cookie, verifies it, and attaches the user to context — or returns 401
requireAuth is applied to all non-auth /api/* routes (tested: GET /api/chats returns 401 without a cookie)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: lib/auth.ts exports requireAuth (createMiddleware<AuthEnv>) and AuthEnv type. Uses getCookie from hono/cookie and jwtVerify from jose. api/routes/auth.ts updated with signin, signout, and me endpoints; authRouter typed as Hono<AuthEnv>. api/app.ts uses a protectedRoutes sub-app (new Hono<AuthEnv>()) with use('/*', requireAuth) to protect all non-auth routes; stub GET /chats handler added for acceptance criteria test.

userStories.title: Auth UI page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean page to sign in or create an account so I can access the app.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /auth renders a page with a sign-in form (email + password + submit) and a toggle to switch to sign-up
Sign-up form has email + password + confirm password fields
Inline validation errors appear for: empty fields, invalid email format, password under 8 characters
Successful sign-in or sign-up redirects the user to /main
Visiting /main without an auth cookie redirects to /auth
Forms use Ark UI components (Field, Input, Button) with UnoCSS styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: islands/AuthForm.tsx is a Preact island with Ark UI-inspired Field/Input/Button primitives (no external Ark dep), client-side validation (empty, invalid email, <8 chars, password mismatch), and fetch calls to /api/auth/signin and /api/auth/signup. routes/auth.tsx uses getAuthUser() server-side to redirect authed users to /main. routes/main.tsx uses getAuthUser() to redirect unauthenticated users to /auth. getAuthUser helper added to lib/auth.ts to parse/verify the JWT cookie from a plain Request object without Hono.

userStories.title: API key management backend
userStories.description: As a user, I want to store API keys for LLM providers so the app can call models on my behalf without exposing keys to the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/settings/api-key accepts { provider: string, key: string } and upserts an encrypted key for the current user
Keys are encrypted using AES-GCM via the Web Crypto API before being stored in user_api_key
GET /api/settings/api-key returns [{ provider, isSet: true/false }] — the raw key is never included
DELETE /api/settings/api-key/:provider removes the stored key for that provider
A getDecryptedKey(userId, provider) helper in lib/keys.ts decrypts and returns the raw key for server-side LLM calls
All routes require auth (requireAuth middleware)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: lib/crypto.ts implements AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt using Web Crypto API (HKDF key derivation from ENCRYPTION_KEY env var; iv prepended to ciphertext; stored as base64url). lib/keys.ts exports getDecryptedKey(userId, provider) which queries userApiKey table and decrypts. api/routes/settings.ts implements PUT /api/settings/api-key (upsert with onConflictDoUpdate), GET /api/settings/api-key (list providers with isSet:true, no raw keys), DELETE /api/settings/api-key/:provider. settingsRouter mounted on protectedRoutes in api/app.ts so all routes require auth.

userStories.title: API key settings UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a settings panel where I can add and remove API keys for different providers so I can manage my credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: A settings panel (modal or drawer) is accessible from the main layout via a settings button
Panel lists supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter) each with an indicator showing whether a key is set
Each provider row has an input to enter/replace the key and a save button
A remove button clears a saved key (with confirmation)
After saving, the indicator updates to show the key is set without a page reload
The raw key value is never displayed after saving — only a masked indicator
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: islands/SettingsPanel.tsx implements a modal with three provider rows (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter). Each row shows isSet indicator (green ● Set / gray ○ Not set), an 'Add key'/'Replace' button that shows a password input inline, and a 'Remove' button with two-step confirmation. Save calls PUT /api/settings/api-key; remove calls DELETE /api/settings/api-key/:provider. Statuses loaded via GET /api/settings/api-key on modal open. The raw key is never redisplayed. routes/main.tsx updated to include SettingsPanel island in the header.

userStories.title: Chat CRUD API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, list, rename, and delete chats so I can organize my conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/chats accepts { title?: string } and creates a new chat owned by the current user; returns the created chat object
GET /api/chats returns all chats for the current user ordered by created_at descending
PATCH /api/chats/:id accepts { title: string } and updates the chat title; returns 404 for chats not owned by the user
DELETE /api/chats/:id deletes the chat and all its nodes and node_metadata rows (cascade); returns 404 for chats not owned by the user
All routes require auth
Zod validates all request bodies
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: chatsRouter in api/routes/chats.ts: POST /chats (create with optional title, returns 201), GET /chats (list by userId ordered by created_at desc), PATCH /chats/:id (update title, 404 if not owned), DELETE /chats/:id (manual cascade: deletes node_metadata → node → chat, 404 if not owned). Replaced stub GET /chats in api/app.ts. Cascade delete uses inArray from drizzle-orm to batch-delete node_metadata for all nodes in the chat.

userStories.title: Node tree loading endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that returns the full flat node list for a chat (with metadata) so the client can reconstruct the tree and render the mindmap.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/chats/:id/nodes returns a flat array of all nodes for the chat
Each node object includes: id, parent_id, role, content, created_at, and a metadata object (provider, model, temperature, token_count) or null if no metadata
Returns 404 if the chat does not exist or is not owned by the current user
Route requires auth
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GET /api/chats/:id/nodes added to chatsRouter. Ownership check via chat.userId. Left join with nodeMetadata on nodeMetadata.nodeId = node.id. metaNodeId used as null sentinel to determine if metadata exists. Response shape: { id, parentId, role, content, createdAt, metadata: { provider, model, temperature, tokenCount } | null }.

userStories.title: Split-view layout and static D3 mindmap
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my conversation tree rendered as a mindmap in a split-view layout so I can understand the branching structure.
acceptanceCriteria: /main route renders a two-column layout: chat panel on the left (60% width), mindmap panel on the right (40% width)
The mindmap panel is a Fresh island (islands/Mindmap.tsx) that accepts a flat nodes array as a prop
D3 d3-hierarchy computes the tree layout from the flat nodes array (parent-pointer reconstruction)
Nodes are rendered as SVG circles, color-coded by role: user=blue, assistant=green, system=gray
Edges between parent and child nodes are rendered as SVG lines or paths
The active node (from TanStack Store) is rendered with a visible highlight (larger radius or ring)
An empty state is shown when the chat has no nodes yet
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Chat list sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a list of my chats in a sidebar and create new ones so I can navigate between conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: A sidebar is visible on /main listing all chats from GET /api/chats via TanStack Query
Each chat row shows the title (or 'Untitled chat' if no title) and the created_at date
Clicking a chat row sets it as the active chat and loads its nodes
A 'New chat' button calls POST /api/chats and navigates to the new chat immediately
A rename action (inline edit or context menu) calls PATCH /api/chats/:id
A delete action (with confirmation) calls DELETE /api/chats/:id and removes it from the list
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Mindmap interactivity: pan, zoom, and node selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pan and zoom the mindmap and click nodes to select them so I can navigate large trees.
acceptanceCriteria: The mindmap SVG supports pan (click-drag) and zoom (scroll wheel)
Pan and zoom events are throttled via TanStack Pacer before the viewport transform in TanStack Store is updated
Clicking any node sets it as the active node in TanStack Store
The active node highlight updates immediately on click without a server round-trip
The chat panel shows the active path (ancestor chain from root to active node) as a linear conversation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Message send endpoint with streaming
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that accepts a message, walks the ancestor context path, calls the LLM via the Vercel AI SDK, streams the response, and persists both the user and assistant nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/chats/:id/messages accepts { parentNodeId: string, content: string, provider: string, model: string }
A user node is created in the database as a child of parentNodeId before the LLM call
The ancestor chain from parentNodeId to root is walked server-side to build the context array (ordered root → parentNodeId)
The Vercel AI SDK streamText is called with the context array and the user's decrypted API key for the specified provider
The response is streamed to the client as text/event-stream
On stream completion, an assistant node is created in the database and a node_metadata row is persisted with provider, model, temperature (default 0.7), and token_count from the SDK usage object
Returns 400 if parentNodeId does not belong to the specified chat
Returns 400 if no API key is set for the specified provider
Route requires auth
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Streaming chat UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type a message and see the assistant's reply stream in token by token so I can have a responsive conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat panel (Fresh island) has a text input and send button at the bottom
Submitting a message calls POST /api/chats/:id/messages with the active node as parentNodeId
Streaming tokens are appended to the assistant message in the UI as they arrive
Token stream updates are batched via TanStack Pacer (max 60fps) to avoid render thrash
The user message appears immediately in the chat panel before the stream starts
The send button is disabled while a stream is in progress
On stream completion, TanStack Query invalidates the node tree query so the mindmap re-renders with the new nodes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Context path token counting and trimming
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to automatically trim the context if it gets too long and show me how many tokens I'm using so I never hit a hard API limit.
acceptanceCriteria: Before calling streamText, the server estimates the token count of the full context path (1 token ≈ 4 characters approximation)
If the path exceeds 45% of the model's context window (default 128k if unknown), the oldest messages are dropped first — but the system node (if any) and the last two exchanges are always preserved
The message endpoint response headers include X-Token-Count and X-Token-Limit so the client can display usage
The chat input area shows a token usage indicator (e.g. '1,240 / 57,600 tokens') updated after each response
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fork conversation from any node
userStories.description: As a user, I want to fork the conversation from any node in the mindmap so I can explore a different direction without losing my current thread.
acceptanceCriteria: Each node in the mindmap has a fork button visible on hover (or right-click context menu)
Clicking fork sets that node as the active node in TanStack Store (setting it as the next parentNodeId)
The chat input area shows a 'Branching from: [first 40 chars of node content]' indicator when a non-leaf node is active
Sending a message from a forked point creates children of that node, which appear as a new branch in the mindmap SVG
The original branch's nodes remain in the tree and are still navigable by clicking them
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: System nodes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to insert system-role messages at any point in the tree to steer the model's behavior for that branch.
acceptanceCriteria: When a node is active, the chat input area has an 'Add system message' button or toggle
Activating it changes the role of the next submission to 'system' instead of 'user'
System nodes are created via POST /api/chats/:id/messages with role: 'system' (the endpoint already supports this from US-016)
System nodes appear in the mindmap with gray fill and a distinct icon (e.g. a settings/gear symbol inside the circle)
System nodes appear in the chat panel with a visually distinct style (gray background, italic text)
System nodes are included in the context path at their correct position during generation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Node metadata display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which model generated an assistant reply and how many tokens it used so I understand the cost and provenance of each response.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering over an assistant node in the mindmap shows a tooltip with: provider, model, temperature, token count
Clicking an assistant node in the chat panel shows an inline metadata row below the message with the same fields
Token count is formatted with commas (e.g. '1,240 tokens')
Metadata is sourced from the node_metadata object already included in the GET /api/chats/:id/nodes response (no extra API calls)
User and system nodes show no metadata panel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Model selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose which model is used for generation so I can switch between providers and models depending on the task.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat settings panel (accessible from the main layout) has a model input field for the chat's default model (stored in chat.default_model via PATCH /api/chats/:id)
The chat input area has a compact model selector (text input or dropdown) that overrides the model for the next message only
Model identifier format is provider/model-name (e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5, openai/gpt-4o, openrouter/meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct)
If no model is set on the chat and no override is provided, SAPLING_DEFAULT_MODEL env var is used as the fallback
The model used for each generation is visible in the node metadata (already stored in node_metadata.model from US-016)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
